element_introduction_order.mastery_context: well-rounded unit, medium-range, high rate of fire foundation for player learning
defensive positioning, melee timing in crowded battles

element_introduction_order.first_appears: early battles, immediately when soldiers fall

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: long-range positioning, cover interaction, instant-death mechanics
area-of-effect tactics, cover-ignoring trajectory, multi-unit damage
flanking tactics, close-quarters dominance, water hazard vulnerability
heavy area damage, extremely slow rate of fire, positioning discipline
precision control subset, alternative strategic approach
score chase incentives, extended campaign sustainability
reliability risk, tactical unit rotation awareness

typical_relief_triggers: successful medic revival of fallen unit
reaching point threshold for bonus medic use
using cover to block long-range attacks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Real-time action with strategy pauses; tension escalates through squad attrition and tactical positioning pressure; relief cycles through successful revivals and unit eliminations. Battle duration varies by campaign mode length.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: bonus objective targets (destroyable by Launcher, Chucker, Blaster classes)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Boot camp tutorial introduces Gunner class and core mechanics (close combat, medic system) in controlled safe environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Campaign progression introduces Launcher, Chucker classes; players learn cover mechanics, area-of-effect tactics, limited resource management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Scorcher and Blaster classes appear; opponents deploy high-risk flank units and instant-death capable weapons; medic reserves become critical
notable_spikes: First encounter with Scorcher unit (most dangerous, fastest-firing, multiple-hit capability, shortest range counter-play required)
Late campaign medic depletion (accumulated unit losses reduce available revives)
Enemy capital city assault (objective completion under pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player chooses 1 of 4 teams before each battle; 3 teams have 5 soldiers with different class combinations, 1 team has 2 commandos with enhanced precision control. Team choice directly impacts tactical approach and unit synergy (e.g., Scorcher flanking pairs vs. Launcher long-range setup).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All campaign battles and multiplayer modes

notable_level_patterns.description: No explicit elimination threshold stated; victory implied through enemy squad depletion and capital capture. Medic limit creates natural attrition ceiling; players must balance revival resource against incoming damage.

Cover blocks Launcher attacks but grenades/dynamite bypass; Scorcher close-range threat forces positioning decisions. Isometric perspective allows multi-directional approach angles; flanking becomes primary tactical depth.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All battles with environmental cover elements

notable_level_patterns.description: Launcher, Scorcher, and Blaster attacks can randomly result in instant-death (skeleton/ash form prevents medic revival). Creates emotional swing tension and unpredictability; loss of high-value unit without recovery option amplifies campaign pressure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late campaign battles featuring these classes
